title: gymnocarpium robertianum subsp. longulum
intro: Gymnocarpium robertianum subsp. longulum, also known as the long-tailed oak fern or long-leaved oak fern, is a subspecies of the Gymnocarpium robertianum plant, a type of fern found in wetlands and woodlands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gymnocarpium robertianum subsp. longulum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and treat respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
